 {"id":"beads_rust-2mwr","title":"[EPIC] ultimate_mcp_server #10: Add AGENTS.md for Agent Guidance","description":"## GitHub Issue Reference
- **Repo:** Dicklesworthstone/ultimate_mcp_server
- **Issue:** #10
- **Reporter:** Saik0s
- **Created:** 2026-01-21

## Problem Statement
No AGENTS.md or AGENT.md exists in this repository. The ecosystem uses AGENTS.md to define safety, toolchain, workflow stages, and quality gates for AI agents.

## Current State
- **No AGENTS.md:** Missing
- **No AGENT.md:** Missing
- Unknown if other agent guidance files exist

## Why This Matters
ultimate_mcp_server is an MCP (Model Context Protocol) server - it's literally designed for AI agents to interact with. Having proper AGENTS.md is critical for:
1. Agents working on the codebase itself
2. Documentation of safe usage patterns
3. Ecosystem consistency

## Implementation Approach
Since there's no existing AGENT.md to reference, we need to create AGENTS.md from scratch with:
1. Project-specific safety rules
2. MCP server development guidelines
3. Testing and quality requirements
4. Common pitfalls to avoid

## Research Needed
- Review existing README.md for project context
- Check if there are any .cursor/rules or similar
- Look at code structure to understand key areas
- Identify MCP-specific safety considerations

## Success Criteria
- AGENTS.md exists at repo root
- Contains safety, workflow, and quality guidance
- Tailored to MCP server development context","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-25T02:45:50.968928322Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-25T04:09:36.821587909Z","external_ref":"github:Dicklesworthstone/ultimate_mcp_server#10","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cli"],"comments":[{"id":184,"issue_id":"beads_rust-2mwr","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Background: AGENTS.md for MCP Servers

ultimate_mcp_server is an MCP (Model Context Protocol) server - literally designed for AI agents to interact with. The irony of it lacking agent guidelines is not lost on us.

## Why This is Important

MCP servers are the interface between AI agents and capabilities. An agent working on an MCP server codebase needs to understand:
1. **Security boundaries** - Servers receive untrusted input from agents
2. **Tool design patterns** - How to add new tools correctly
3. **Error handling conventions** - Consistent error formats
4. **Resource management** - Don't leak memory or connections

## Server vs Client Considerations

Unlike the MCP client (which orchestrates), the server:
- Exposes tools that agents call
- Must validate ALL inputs (agents send arbitrary data)
- Must be defensive against malformed requests
- Should fail gracefully with clear error messages

## Content Focus

The AGENTS.md should emphasize:
- Input validation (NEVER trust agent input)
- Resource limits (timeouts, memory bounds)
- Secrets handling (don't log API keys)
- Testing requirements (tools must have tests)

## From Scratch

Unlike mcp_client, this repo has no existing AGENT.md. We'll create AGENTS.md from scratch based on:
- README review
- Code structure analysis
- MCP protocol best practices
- General server security principles","created_at":"2026-01-25T04:09:36Z"}]}
 {"id":"beads_rust-2mwr.1","title":"Research ultimate_mcp_server codebase for AGENTS.md content","description":"## Context
Part of ultimate_mcp_server #10 fix. Research the codebase to inform AGENTS.md content.

## Research Tasks

### 1. Project Overview
- Read README.md for project purpose and structure
- Identify main features and capabilities
- Understand technology stack

### 2. Code Structure
- Map directory structure
- Identify key modules/components
- Find entry points and critical paths

### 3. Existing Guidelines
- Check for .cursor/rules, .github/CONTRIBUTING.md
- Look for code comments with safety notes
- Check for existing documentation

### 4. MCP-Specific Considerations
Since this is an MCP server, identify:
- Tool definitions and handlers
- Security boundaries
- Resource access patterns
- Error handling conventions

### 5. Safety Concerns
For an MCP server, critical safety areas:
- Input validation (agents send arbitrary data)
- Resource limits (prevent DoS)
- Secrets handling (API keys, tokens)
- File system access controls
- External API interactions

## Deliverable
Summary document with:
- Project overview (1 paragraph)
- Key directories and their purposes
- Safety rules to include
- Workflow recommendations
- Quality gates appropriate for the project","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-25T02:46:50.611527994Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-25T09:53:41.771309936Z","closed_at":"2026-01-25T09:53:41.771291662Z","close_reason":"Research summary added in ultimate_mcp_server/AGENTS_RESEARCH.md","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cli"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"beads_rust-2mwr.1","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-2mwr","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-25T02:46:50.611527994Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":187,"issue_id":"beads_rust-2mwr.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Research summary written to /data/projects/ultimate_mcp_server/AGENTS_RESEARCH.md (overview, key dirs, safety rules, workflows, quality gates).","created_at":"2026-01-25T09:53:37Z"}]}
 {"id":"beads_rust-2mwr.2","title":"Create AGENTS.md for ultimate_mcp_server","description":"## Context
Part of ultimate_mcp_server #10 fix. Create AGENTS.md tailored to MCP server development.

## AGENTS.md Structure

### Header
```markdown
# AGENTS.md - Ultimate MCP Server

> Agent guidelines for developing and maintaining this MCP server.
```

### 1. Project Overview
Brief description of what the server does and key features.

### 2. Safety Rules (Critical for MCP Servers)
```markdown
## Safety First

### Input Validation
- ALWAYS validate all tool inputs
- NEVER trust data from agents/clients
- Sanitize file paths (no path traversal)
- Validate URLs and external references

### Resource Protection
- Respect rate limits
- Implement timeouts for external calls
- Don't exhaust memory or disk
- Log suspicious activity

### Secrets Handling
- NEVER log API keys or tokens
- Use environment variables for secrets
- Don't expose secrets in error messages
```

### 3. Development Workflow
- How to run locally
- How to test changes
- How to add new tools

### 4. Quality Gates
- Tests must pass
- Type checking (if TypeScript)
- Linting requirements
- Security review for new tools

### 5. Common Pitfalls
MCP-specific issues to avoid:
- Blocking the event loop
- Memory leaks in long-running handlers
- Inconsistent error formats
- Missing tool descriptions

## File to Create
- ultimate_mcp_server/AGENTS.md

## Verification
- Accurate to codebase
- Actionable guidelines
- Renders correctly on GitHub","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-25T02:47:54.304721012Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-25T09:54:51.318565742Z","closed_at":"2026-01-25T09:54:51.318546335Z","close_reason":"Added AGENTS.md in ultimate_mcp_server","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cli"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"beads_rust-2mwr.2","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-2mwr","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-25T02:47:54.304721012Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-2mwr.2","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-2mwr.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-25T02:50:15.561461168Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":188,"issue_id":"beads_rust-2mwr.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Created /data/projects/ultimate_mcp_server/AGENTS.md with overview, safety rules, workflows, and quality gates.","created_at":"2026-01-25T09:54:46Z"}]}

The new AGENTS.md includes:
- Safety rules tailored for MCP server development
- Input validation and resource protection guidelines
- Development workflow and quality gates
- Common pitfalls to avoid

Thanks for flagging the missing guidelines!
```

## Verification Before Closing
1. AGENTS.md exists at repo root
2. Content is accurate and helpful
3. Commit pushed to main

## Command
```bash
gh issue close 10 -R Dicklesworthstone/ultimate_mcp_server -c "[message above]"

@@ -144,7 +144,7 @@
 {"id":"beads_rust-36jt","title":"Release automation: GitHub Actions multi-platform builds","description":"# Release Automation via GitHub Actions

## Scope
Create GitHub Actions workflow for automated multi-platform releases with signing and checksums.

## Deliverables
- Multi-platform build matrix: linux-x86_64, linux-arm64, macos-x86_64, macos-arm64, windows-x86_64
- Automatic SHA256 checksum generation (.sha256 files)
- Ed25519 signature generation (.sig files)
- Release asset naming convention: br-vX.Y.Z-{os}-{arch}.tar.gz
- Automatic changelog generation from closed beads
- Draft release creation on tag push
- Binary size optimization (LTO, strip)
- Cross-compilation via cross-rs

## Workflow Triggers
- on: push: tags: ['v*'] - Create release
- on: pull_request - Build only (no release)
- on: workflow_dispatch - Manual trigger with version input

## Acceptance Criteria
- Pushing vX.Y.Z tag creates draft GitHub release with all binaries
- Each binary has matching .sha256 and .sig files
- Release notes contain changelog from closed beads
- Build completes in <30 minutes
- All binaries are fully static (no libc dependency on Linux)
- macOS binaries are notarized (if possible)

## Unit Tests
- test_changelog_generation: verify changelog format from beads
- test_asset_naming: verify naming convention compliance
- test_checksum_format: verify .sha256 file format

## E2E Tests (tests/e2e_release.rs)
- e2e_release_dry_run: simulate release workflow, verify artifacts
- e2e_checksum_verification: download release, verify checksums match
- e2e_signature_verification: download release, verify Ed25519 signatures
- e2e_changelog_content: verify changelog includes expected closed beads

## CI/CD Integration Tests
- workflow_syntax_valid: validate .github/workflows/release.yml syntax
- workflow_matrix_complete: verify all platforms in matrix
- workflow_secrets_documented: verify required secrets documented

## Logging Requirements
- [BUILD] Building for linux-x86_64...
- [SIGN] Generating Ed25519 signature...
- [UPLOAD] Uploading br-v1.0.0-linux-x86_64.tar.gz (27.4MB)
- [RELEASE] Draft release v1.0.0 created with 10 assets","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CopperMarsh","created_at":"2026-01-21T00:50:18.438708303Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T00:58:17.714366301Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T00:58:17.714285709Z","close_reason":"Implementation complete - release.yml includes all required features: multi-platform builds, SHA256 checksums, Ed25519 minisign signatures, changelog from closed beads. Already committed in 88e4c96.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"beads_rust-36jt","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-7nh","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-21T00:50:18.500076649Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":151,"issue_id":"beads_rust-36jt","author":"CopperMarsh","text":"## Implementation Complete: GitHub Actions Release Automation

### What Was Added

1. **Ed25519 Signature Generation**
   - Installed minisign on Linux (via tarball) and macOS (via brew)
   - Added signing step using `MINISIGN_SECRET_KEY` GitHub secret
   - Generates `.minisig` files for each archive
   - Added verification instructions in release notes
   - Public key placeholder: `RWSp4vEOdKsY8e95W9/4eLrSJ2B2GHv4U+CKMBXqRX3JhPrPn8J0DWBG`

2. **Changelog from Closed Beads**
   - Parses `.beads/issues.jsonl` to extract closed beads since last release tag
   - Uses jq to filter by `closed_at` or `updated_at` date
   - Groups beads under "### Closed Beads" section in release notes
   - Falls back to last 20 beads if no previous tag exists

### What Was Already Present
- Multi-platform build matrix (linux x64/arm64, macos x64/arm64, windows x64)
- SHA256 checksum generation and verification
- Release naming convention: br-vX.Y.Z-{platform}.tar.gz
- Workflow triggers: tag push (v*), workflow_dispatch
- Binary optimization: LTO, strip, opt-level=z
- crates.io publishing

### Required GitHub Secrets
- `MINISIGN_SECRET_KEY`: Ed25519 private key for signing (optional - skipped if not set)
- `CRATES_IO_TOKEN`: For crates.io publishing (already present)

### Setup Instructions for Ed25519 Signing
```bash
# Generate key pair (do once)
minisign -G -s minisign.key -p minisign.pub

# Add minisign.key contents to GitHub secret MINISIGN_SECRET_KEY
# Update public key in release.yml to match minisign.pub
```

-- CopperMarsh

Total model tests now 61 (was 7).","compaction_level":0,"compacted_at_commit":"","original_size":0}
 {"id":"beads_rust-37qt","title":"[EPIC] beads_rust #25: Add Line Wrapping Option for Pretty View","description":"## GitHub Issue Reference
- **Repo:** Dicklesworthstone/beads_rust
- **Issue:** #25
- **Reporter:** dgunay
- **Created:** 2026-01-24

## Problem Statement
In br's pretty/rich output mode, commands like `show`, `comments`, `list` truncate long lines to fit terminal width. Users can't see full content without switching to `--json` or `--no-color` mode.

## User's Workaround Acknowledged
The reporter notes that `bv` (the TUI companion) makes this "a non-issue" but requests wrapping for users who don't want to install bv.

## Solution Options Considered

### Option 1: Add --wrap flag
Add `--wrap` or `--no-truncate` flag to relevant commands.
- Pros: Explicit user control, backward compatible
- Cons: More flags to maintain

### Option 2: Smart truncation with expand hint
Keep truncation but show "[...] use --json for full" hint
- Pros: Maintains clean output, teaches users
- Cons: Doesn't fully solve the problem

### Option 3: Auto-detect and wrap
Detect when content would truncate and auto-wrap
- Pros: Just works
- Cons: May break some workflows that parse output

## Recommended Approach
Option 1: Add `--wrap` flag. It's explicit, backward compatible, and gives users control.

## Affected Commands
- `br show`
- `br list`
- `br comments`
- Possibly `br search`, `br ready`, `br blocked`

## Success Criteria
- `br show --wrap ID` shows full content with line wrapping
- Default behavior unchanged (truncation for clean output)
- Works correctly with different terminal widths

## Priority Rationale
P3 because:
- bv provides a workaround
- `--json` provides full content
- Nice-to-have UX improvement, not blocking functionality","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-25T02:28:54.776184953Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-25T04:07:54.320996739Z","external_ref":"github:Dicklesworthstone/beads_rust#25","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cli","output"],"comments":[{"id":181,"issue_id":"beads_rust-37qt","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Background: UX Polish for CLI Tools

This is a lower-priority UX enhancement. The reporter acknowledges bv (the TUI) solves this, but wants CLI-only users to have a good experience too.

## Design Philosophy

beads_rust follows the Unix philosophy of composable tools:
- `br` for CLI operations (quick, scriptable)
- `bv` for interactive TUI (exploration, visualization)
- `--json` for machine parsing

Truncation is intentional for clean terminal output. Long content should use bv or --json. However, adding --wrap as an opt-in gives users explicit control.

## Implementation Approach

The key insight is that wrapping with ANSI colors is tricky. We need to:
1. Calculate visual width (ignoring escape codes)
2. Wrap at the right character boundaries
3. Preserve color codes across line breaks

The `textwrap` crate handles plain text. For ANSI, we may need `console` or `strip-ansi-escapes` crates.

## Priority Rationale

P3 because:
- Clear workarounds exist (bv, --json)
- Not blocking any workflows
- Nice-to-have polish, not must-have functionality

## Success Metrics

- Users can see full content without switching tools
- Default behavior unchanged (no breaking changes)
- Performance impact negligible","created_at":"2026-01-25T04:07:54Z"}]}
-{"id":"beads_rust-37qt.1","title":"Add --wrap flag to br output formatter","description":"## Context
Part of beads_rust #25 fix. Add a --wrap flag to enable line wrapping instead of truncation.

## Implementation Plan

### 1. Find the output formatting code
Likely locations:
- src/cli/output.rs or src/output/
- src/cli/commands/show.rs (per-command)
- Look for terminal width detection (likely uses `terminal_size` crate)

### 2. Understand current truncation logic
- How is terminal width detected?
- Where is content truncated?
- Is there a central formatter or per-command logic?

### 3. Add --wrap flag
Add to relevant CLI commands:
```rust
#[arg(long, help = "Wrap long lines instead of truncating")]
wrap: bool,
```

### 4. Implement wrapping logic
Options:
- Use `textwrap` crate for word-aware wrapping
- Simple character wrapping for code/IDs
- Preserve indentation on wrapped lines

### 5. Update formatter to respect flag
```rust
if args.wrap {
    wrap_text(content, terminal_width)
} else {
    truncate_text(content, terminal_width)
}
```

## Technical Considerations
- Colored output: wrapping must preserve ANSI codes
- Multi-byte characters: use unicode-width for correct wrapping
- Tables: may need different handling (wrap cell content vs entire row)

## Files to Modify
- src/cli/mod.rs (add global flag or per-command)
- src/output/formatter.rs (implement wrapping)
- Cargo.toml (add textwrap dependency if needed)","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-25T02:29:39.611282444Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-25T02:29:39.611282444Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cli","output"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"beads_rust-37qt.1","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-37qt","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-25T02:29:39.611282444Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

Options:
- Use `textwrap` crate for word-aware wrapping
- Simple character wrapping for code/IDs
- Preserve indentation on wrapped lines

### 5. Update formatter to respect flag
```rust
if args.wrap {
    wrap_text(content, terminal_width)
} else {
    truncate_text(content, terminal_width)
}
```

## Technical Considerations
- Colored output: wrapping must preserve ANSI codes
- Multi-byte characters: use unicode-width for correct wrapping
- Tables: may need different handling (wrap cell content vs entire row)

## Files to Modify
- src/cli/mod.rs (add global flag or per-command)
- src/output/formatter.rs (implement wrapping)
- Cargo.toml (add textwrap dependency if needed)","status":"in_progress","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-25T02:29:39.611282444Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-25T15:32:52.028369023Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cli","output"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"beads_rust-37qt.1","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-37qt","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-25T02:29:39.611282444Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

3. Test with --wrap on wide terminal (200 cols)

5. Test with ANSI colors enabled

### Unit Tests (src/output/formatter_test.rs or similar)

   - Unicode text → correct character boundaries
   - Text with existing newlines → preserved and additional wrapping

2. **Truncation unchanged:**

   - Truncation with ANSI codes

### Integration Tests
1. **br show --wrap:**
   - Create issue with long description
   - Verify wrapped output contains all content
   - Verify line lengths ≤ terminal width

2. **br list --wrap:**
   - Create multiple issues with long titles
   - Verify table renders correctly
   - Verify all content visible

3. **Snapshot tests:**
   - Capture expected output for various scenarios
   - Detect unintended changes

### Edge Cases
- Empty content
- Single very long word (no wrap points)
- Content that's exactly terminal width
- Terminal width of 20 (very narrow)
- Terminal width of 1000 (very wide)

- src/output/mod.rs or tests/output_test.rs
- tests/cli_integration_test.rs","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-25T02:31:11.373640511Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-25T02:32:58.657192464Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["output","tests"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"beads_rust-37qt.3","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-37qt","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-25T02:31:11.373640511Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-37qt.3","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-37qt.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-25T02:32:58.654434480Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"beads_rust-3qud","title":"[EPIC] ultimate_mcp_client #7: Add Standard AGENTS.md File","description":"## GitHub Issue Reference
- **Repo:** Dicklesworthstone/ultimate_mcp_client
- **Issue:** #7
- **Reporter:** Saik0s
- **Created:** 2026-01-21

## Problem Statement
The repo has AGENT.md (107KB comprehensive document) and .cursor/rules, but no AGENTS.md. The ecosystem uses AGENTS.md as the standard entry point for agent safety, workflow, and quality gates.

## Current State
- **AGENT.md exists:** 107KB comprehensive "Unified Memory System & Agent Master Loop" document
- **No AGENTS.md:** Missing standard entry point file
- **.cursor/rules exists:** Editor-specific agent rules

## Analysis
The existing AGENT.md is extensive and valuable, but:
1. AGENTS.md is the ecosystem convention
2. Agents look for AGENTS.md first
3. The singular AGENT.md may be overlooked

## Solution Options

### Option A: Rename AGENT.md to AGENTS.md
- Simple file rename
- Preserves all content
- May break existing links/references

### Option B: Create AGENTS.md that references AGENT.md
```markdown
# AGENTS.md

See [AGENT.md](AGENT.md) for comprehensive agent guidelines.

Quick reference:
- Safety: [link to section]
- Workflow: [link to section]
- Quality gates: [link to section]
```

### Option C: Create condensed AGENTS.md alongside AGENT.md
- New AGENTS.md with essential rules
- Keep AGENT.md for deep documentation
- May cause confusion with two files

## Recommended Approach
Option B: Create AGENTS.md as a pointer/summary. This:
- Adds the standard entry point
- Preserves the valuable AGENT.md
- Doesn't break existing references
- Provides quick-reference value

## Success Criteria
- AGENTS.md exists at repo root
- AGENTS.md directs agents to AGENT.md
- Includes essential safety/workflow summary","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-25T02:39:54.893323648Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-25T04:08:52.915049978Z","external_ref":"github:Dicklesworthstone/ultimate_mcp_client#7","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cli"],"comments":[{"id":183,"issue_id":"beads_rust-3qud","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Background: AGENTS.md Ecosystem Convention

AGENTS.md is the standard entry point for AI agents working on a codebase. It defines:
- Safety rules (what not to do)
- Workflow stages (how to work)
- Quality gates (what must pass)
- Project-specific guidance

## Current State Analysis

ultimate_mcp_client has AGENT.md (singular, 107KB) - a comprehensive "Unified Memory System & Agent Master Loop" document. This is valuable but:
1. Agents look for AGENTS.md (plural) first
2. 107KB is overwhelming for quick reference
3. The naming doesn't match ecosystem convention

## Resolution: Pointer File

Rather than rename (which might break references) or duplicate content, we'll create AGENTS.md as a pointer:
- Quick-reference safety rules
- Links to detailed sections in AGENT.md
- Serves as the standard entry point

This follows the DRY principle while meeting ecosystem conventions.

## MCP Client Context

This is an MCP client - it connects to MCP servers and orchestrates tool calls. Safety considerations include:
- Don't leak credentials to servers
- Handle server errors gracefully
- Respect rate limits and resource bounds

The AGENTS.md should reflect these client-specific concerns.","created_at":"2026-01-25T04:08:52Z"}]}

 {"id":"beads_rust-3qud.2","title":"Create AGENTS.md for ultimate_mcp_client","description":"## Context
Part of ultimate_mcp_client #7 fix. Create the AGENTS.md file.

## AGENTS.md Structure

### Header
```markdown
# AGENTS.md - Ultimate MCP Client

> Standard agent guidelines for AI coding assistants.
> For comprehensive documentation, see [AGENT.md](AGENT.md).
```

### Quick Reference Sections

1. **Safety First**
   - Critical rules from AGENT.md
   - Don't break user data
   - Don't expose secrets
   - Respect rate limits

2. **Workflow Overview**
   - Reference Agent Master Loop stages
   - Link to detailed sections

3. **Quality Gates**
   - What must pass before commit
   - Testing requirements
   - Review checklist

4. **Project-Specific Rules**
   - MCP client-specific considerations
   - API usage patterns
   - Error handling conventions

### Cross-References
Link to:
- AGENT.md (full documentation)
- .cursor/rules (editor integration)
- README.md (project overview)

## Template
Use ecosystem-standard AGENTS.md format:
- Brief, scannable
- Links to detailed docs
- Action-oriented ("Do X", "Don't Y")

## File to Create
- ultimate_mcp_client/AGENTS.md

## Verification
- File renders correctly on GitHub
- Links work
- Content is accurate to AGENT.md","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-25T02:41:36.474659876Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-25T09:58:43.040470601Z","closed_at":"2026-01-25T09:58:43.040452257Z","close_reason":"Added AGENTS.md in ultimate_mcp_client","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cli"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"beads_rust-3qud.2","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-3qud","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-25T02:41:36.474659876Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-3qud.2","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-3qud.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-25T02:43:52.614931027Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":190,"issue_id":"beads_rust-3qud.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Created /data/projects/ultimate_mcp_client/AGENTS.md (quick reference + links to AGENT.md/.cursor rules + workflow + quality gates).","created_at":"2026-01-25T09:58:38Z"}]}

 {"id":"beads_rust-3stn","title":"Integrate rich output into ready command","description":"## Command: br ready

### Traffic Level: HIGH  
The primary command for finding actionable work. Agents use this constantly to find what to work on next.

### Current Implementation
Location: src/cli/commands/ready.rs
Output: Table of issues with no blocking dependencies

### Integration Steps
1. Use IssueTable with highlight_ready(true)
2. Add 'ready' visual indicator (✓ or green highlight)
3. Show blocking count in additional column
4. Consider adding priority sorting emphasis

### Visual Enhancement
Ready issues should STAND OUT:
- Green left border or background tint
- Bold ID and title
- Checkmark icon prefix

### Table Columns
- ID (bold, green-tinted)
- Priority (heat-mapped)
- Title
- Type
- Labels
- Age (how long has it been ready?)

### Agent Optimization
The --robot flag must produce stable, parseable output:
- IDs one per line, or
- JSON array of IDs
No visual formatting that could break parsing.

### Sorting
Maintain current sort: priority desc, then created asc
Visual priority distinction helps humans scan quickly.

---

## Testing Requirements

### Unit Tests
Location: tests/cli/ready_tests.rs

```rust
#[test]
fn test_ready_table_uses_issue_table_component() {
    // Verify that ready command uses IssueTable internally
    // and respects highlight_ready(true) setting
}

#[test]
fn test_ready_json_output_unchanged() {
    // Compare JSON output byte-for-byte with baseline fixture
    let current = run_ready_json();
    let baseline = load_fixture("tests/fixtures/json_baseline/ready.json");
    assert_json_eq!(current, baseline);
}

#[test]
fn test_ready_respects_robot_flag() {
    // --robot should produce parseable output without ANSI
    let output = run_ready_robot();
    assert!(!contains_ansi_codes(&output));
}

#[test]
fn test_ready_highlights_actionable_issues() {
    // In rich mode, ready issues should have green styling
    let ctx = OutputContext::rich();
    let table = IssueTable::new(&issues, &ctx).highlight_ready(true);
    // Verify styling applied
}
```

### Integration Tests
Location: tests/integration/ready_integration.rs

```rust
#[test]
fn test_ready_command_e2e() {
    // Full CLI invocation with real database
    let result = Command::new("br")
        .args(&["ready", "--json"])
        .output();
    assert!(result.is_ok());
}

#[test]
fn test_ready_filters_blocked_issues() {
    // Create test issues with dependencies
    // Verify ready only shows unblocked issues
}
```

### E2E Test Script
Location: tests/e2e/ready_e2e.sh

```bash
#!/bin/bash
set -euo pipefail
source "$(dirname "$0")/../common/test_helpers.sh"

log_info "=== E2E: Ready Command ==="

# Setup
setup_test_db
create_test_issues_with_deps

# Test 1: Basic ready command
log_step "Testing basic ready command"
OUTPUT=$(br ready 2>&1)
assert_contains "$OUTPUT" "beads_rust-" "Should show issue IDs"
log_pass "Basic ready works"

# Test 2: JSON output unchanged
log_step "Testing JSON backward compatibility"
CURRENT=$(br ready --json | jq -S .)
BASELINE=$(cat tests/fixtures/json_baseline/ready.json | jq -S .)
assert_json_eq "$CURRENT" "$BASELINE" "JSON output should match baseline"
log_pass "JSON backward compatibility verified"

# Test 3: Robot mode
log_step "Testing robot mode"
ROBOT_OUTPUT=$(br ready --robot 2>&1)
assert_no_ansi "$ROBOT_OUTPUT" "Robot mode should have no ANSI codes"
log_pass "Robot mode clean"

# Test 4: Rich mode with TTY simulation
log_step "Testing rich mode (simulated TTY)"
RICH_OUTPUT=$(script -q /dev/null br ready 2>&1 || true)
# Verify we got some output (rich mode)
assert_not_empty "$RICH_OUTPUT" "Rich mode should produce output"
log_pass "Rich mode works"

log_success "=== Ready command E2E: ALL PASSED ==="
```

### Logging Requirements
- Log entry when ready command starts with filter parameters
- Log count of issues found vs filtered out
- Log rendering mode selected (rich/plain/json)
- Log completion time for performance tracking","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T20:31:39.799373905Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-20T05:54:03.977890185Z","closed_at":"2026-01-20T05:54:03.977777733Z","close_reason":"ready command fully integrated with IssueTable in src/cli/commands/ready.rs lines 98-115","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"beads_rust-3stn","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-36dk","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-19T20:32:26.836620619Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-3stn","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-zbjk","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-19T20:31:39.814984830Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
